titleStmt.title: Letter from John Pedder to Matthew Nathan, the Under-Secretary for Ireland, 6 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Pedder

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by John Pedder (1869-1956), assistant secretary at the Home Office in London. The letter is addressed to the under-secretary, Dublin Castle. Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), served as the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.The letter concerns a report on the application of Russian born Max Hinden (b. 1886), for a Certificate of Naturalisation.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6291

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE, WHITEHALL. 6th. April. 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to refer to
your letter of 20th. April. 1915. (6384), forwarding
a report on the application of Max Hinden for a
Certificate of Naturalization, and to enquire whether
the report still holds good. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John Pedder The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle. Chief Commissioner
D.M.P.,
For report
10:4:16
